summary.brief: Arlene Wickham v. Jean Hummel. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Wickham ultimately underwent a below-the-knee amputation following her knee replacement surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury found Hummel, a registered nurse, 100% at fault for injuries Wickham sustained.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Wickham underwent left knee replacement surgery at North Kansas City Hospital on February 17, 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Smith performed the surgery, which concluded at 12:39 p.m.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Smith inadvertently cut Wickham’s anterior tibial artery, which runs behind the knee joint.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The bleeding caused by this cut went undetected and untreated for approximately two days.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Wickham required emergency surgery to repair the cut in an attempt to save the function of her leg.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After ten months of extensive rehabilitation efforts, which were unable to restore full function to her leg, Wickham underwent a left below-the-knee amputation in December 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Smith wrote an order that nurses were to perform neurovascular assessments every four hours for 24-hours following surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hummel was a nurse at North Kansas City Hospital who worked the day shift, 7:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m., on February 18, 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hummel assessed Wickham’s dorsiflexion and plantar flexion, which test certain nerve function, as “poor.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hummel did not report Wickham’s status to Dr. Smith.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The physical therapist noted Wickham was experiencing calf soreness, which is a possible sign of a blood clot.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Calf pain is not expected after a total knee replacement.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Wickham’s reported pain increased throughout the morning. Wickham rated her pain as a “2” on a 10-point scale at 6:10 a.m., “9” at 8:28 a.m. and 11:38 a.m., and “10” at 12:18 p.m.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hummel did not notify Dr. Smith of Wickham’s increased pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hummel claims that she spoke with a receptionist from Dr. Smith’s office for approval to get an ultrasound but did not speak with Dr. Smith directly.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Smith denies having knowledge of any call from Hummel, and Hummel did not document the call in Wickham’s chart.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hummel did not document performing any neurovascular assessments in the morning after 8:00 a.m. throughout the afternoon.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hummel was concerned about Wickham’s condition throughout the day, and at 5:55 p.m., Hummel contacted the “STAT team” because Wickham had decreased urine output and had not voided during the entirety of Hummel’s shift.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hummel called Dr. Smith around 6:30 p.m., the first recorded time she contacted him all day.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hummel’s charting on February 18 was so deficient that when she came in for her shift on February 19, her supervisor told her to get her charts up-to-date.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court’s amended judgment is affirmed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Postoperative care following left knee replacement surgery
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent left knee replacement surgery on February 17, 2016. Post-surgery, the anterior tibial artery was inadvertently cut, leading to undetected bleeding.

focused_exam.neurovascular: Poor dorsiflexion and plantar flexion noted at 8:00 a.m. on February 18, 2016

requestables.name: Ultrasound
requestables.description: Ultrasound to assess for blood clots or bleeding

requestables.name: Ultrasound of Lower Extremity
requestables.description: Ultrasound to assess for vascular injury

timeline_phases.name: Postoperative Monitoring
timeline_phases.description: Initial postoperative period following knee replacement

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient showing signs of neurovascular compromise post-surgery
decision_points.prompt: What should the nurse do upon noticing poor neurovascular function?

options.clinical_reasoning: Timely notification could prevent further complications
Delay in notification can lead to worsening condition

actual_action_defendant.description: Nurse did not notify Dr. Smith immediately
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Nurse did not perceive immediate need

expected_action_court.description: Immediate notification to Dr. Smith was required

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to notify led to delayed intervention

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent below-the-knee amputation following complications
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of limb, extensive rehabilitation required

key_findings: Nurse Hummel found 100% at fault for failing to notify physician
Causation established - earlier intervention could have prevented amputation

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to notify physician and perform necessary imaging
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely intervention could have preserved limb function

allegations.allegation: Failure to notify physician of deteriorating condition
Failure to notify physician of deteriorating condition

court_findings.finding: Nurse breached standard of care by failing to notify physician
Nurse Hummel breached duty of care
